         min_prevalence_pct, trough_year)

change_file <- file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_change_over_time_K", chosen_K, ".csv"))
write_csv(overall_change_export, change_file)
cat("\n\nDetailed change table saved to:", change_file, "\n")

yoy_file <- file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_year_over_year_change_K", chosen_K, ".csv"))
write_csv(yoy_change %>%
            mutate(yoy_pct = round(yoy_pct, 2)),
          yoy_file)
cat("Year-over-year change saved to:", yoy_file, "\n")

24. Topic Prevalence Stats Table

prevalence_summary <- doc_topics %>%
  select(starts_with("Topic")) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "topic", values_to = "mean_proportion") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_num   = as.numeric(str_remove(topic, "Topic")),
    label       = topic_labels[topic_num],
    percentage  = round(mean_proportion * 100, 2),
    rank        = rank(-mean_proportion)
  ) %>%
  arrange(rank) %>%
  select(rank, label, percentage)

print(prevalence_summary)

25. Article Type Distribution per Topic

Which article types cluster into which topics?

type_topic <- topic_article_link %>%
  filter(!is.na(Article_Type)) %>%
  group_by(topic_num, topic_label, Article_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_proportion = mean(proportion),
    n_articles = n_distinct(filename),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(topic_num, desc(mean_proportion))

# Show top 3 types per topic
type_topic %>%
  group_by(topic_num, topic_label) %>%
  slice_max(mean_proportion, n = 3) %>%
  print(n = chosen_K * 3)

26. Topic-Article Type Heatmap

# Build a matrix of mean topic proportion by article type
heatmap_data <- topic_article_link %>%
  filter(!is.na(Article_Type), Article_Type != "Front Matter") %>%
  group_by(topic_num, Article_Type) %>%
  summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Article_Type, values_from = mean_prop, values_fill = 0)

heatmap_matrix <- as.matrix(heatmap_data %>% select(-topic_num))
rownames(heatmap_matrix) <- paste0("T", heatmap_data$topic_num, ": ",
                                    str_trunc(topic_labels[heatmap_data$topic_num], 25))

pheatmap(
  heatmap_matrix,
  main = "Topic Affinity by Article Type",
  color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "#FDDBC7", "#CD661D", "#8B4513"))(50),
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_format = "%.2f",
  fontsize_number = 7,
  fontsize_row = 9,
  fontsize_col = 9,
  angle_col = 45
)

27. Stacked Area Chart - Topic Composition Over Time

stacked_data <- make.dt(stm_model, meta = meta) %>%
  select(starts_with("Topic"), year) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("Topic"), names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_num = as.numeric(str_remove(topic, "Topic")),
    label = topic_labels[topic_num]
  )

ggplot(stacked_data, aes(x = year, y = prevalence, fill = reorder(label, -topic_num))) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.8, color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Topic Composition Over Time",
    subtitle = "How the discourse mix shifts across years",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Topic Proportion",
    fill = "Topic"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3", direction = -1)

28. Topic Co-occurrence Within Articles

Which topics tend to appear together in the same article? Articles that are high in one topic may also be high in another, revealing thematic connections.

# Correlation matrix of topic proportions across documents
topic_prop_matrix <- doc_topics %>%
  select(starts_with("Topic"))

topic_cor <- cor(topic_prop_matrix)
rownames(topic_cor) <- paste0("T", 1:chosen_K, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels, 20))
colnames(topic_cor) <- paste0("T", 1:chosen_K)

pheatmap(
  topic_cor,
  main = "Topic Co-occurrence (Correlation Matrix)",
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(50),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_format = "%.2f",
  fontsize_number = 8,
  fontsize_row = 9,
  fontsize_col = 9,
  breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 51)
)

29. Exclusivity vs Semantic Coherence

This plot helps evaluate topic quality. Topics in the upper-right are both coherent (words make sense together) and exclusive (words are unique to that topic). Labeled for easy identification.

topic_qual <- data.frame(
  topic = 1:chosen_K,
  label = topic_labels,
  coherence = semanticCoherence(stm_model, docs),
  exclusivity = exclusivity(stm_model)
)

ggplot(topic_qual, aes(x = coherence, y = exclusivity)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "#CD661D") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("T", topic)), vjust = -1, size = 3.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = str_trunc(label, 25)), vjust = 2, size = 2.5,
            color = "grey40") +
  labs(
    title = "Topic Quality: Exclusivity vs Semantic Coherence",
    subtitle = "Upper-right = best topics (coherent + distinctive)",
    x = "Semantic Coherence",
    y = "Exclusivity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  )

30. Dominant Topic Distribution

How are articles distributed across topics? Are some topics dominating the corpus?

dom_summary <- dominant_topics %>%
  count(dominant_topic, topic_label) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100)

ggplot(dom_summary, aes(x = reorder(topic_label, n), y = n, fill = pct)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", round(pct, 1), "%)")),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Articles per Dominant Topic",
    subtitle = "Each article assigned to its strongest topic",
    x = NULL, y = "Number of Articles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#FDDBC7", high = "#8B4513") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))

31. Topic-by-Volume Heatmap

Shows every topic’s prevalence in every volume at a glance. Spot where topics spike, fade, or first emerge.

vol_topic_data <- make.dt(stm_model, meta = meta) %>%
  select(starts_with("Topic"), volume_num) %>%
  filter(!is.na(volume_num)) %>%
  group_by(volume_num) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

vol_matrix <- as.matrix(vol_topic_data %>% select(-volume_num))
rownames(vol_matrix) <- paste0("Vol ", vol_topic_data$volume_num)
colnames(vol_matrix) <- paste0("T", 1:chosen_K, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels, 20))

pheatmap(
  t(vol_matrix),
  main = "Topic Prevalence by Volume",
  color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "#FDDBC7", "#CD661D", "#8B4513"))(100),
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  display_numbers = FALSE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 9,
  angle_col = 45,
  border_color = "grey90"
)

32. Author-Topic Analysis

Which authors write most about which topics? Filters to authors with multiple articles for meaningful patterns.

# Find authors with enough articles to analyze
author_counts <- dominant_topics %>%
  filter(!is.na(Author), Author != "Unknown") %>%
  count(Author, sort = TRUE)

# Use top 20 most prolific authors
top_authors <- author_counts %>%
  head(20) %>%
  pull(Author)

author_topic_data <- topic_article_link %>%
  filter(Author %in% top_authors) %>%
  group_by(Author, topic_num) %>%
  summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = topic_num, values_from = mean_prop, values_fill = 0)

# Ensure all K topics are present as columns
for (k in 1:chosen_K) {
  if (!as.character(k) %in% names(author_topic_data)) {
    author_topic_data[[as.character(k)]] <- 0
  }
}
author_topic_data <- author_topic_data %>%
  select(Author, all_of(as.character(1:chosen_K)))

author_matrix <- as.matrix(author_topic_data %>% select(-Author))
rownames(author_matrix) <- author_topic_data$Author
colnames(author_matrix) <- paste0("T", 1:chosen_K, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels, 18))

pheatmap(
  author_matrix,
  main = paste0("Author-Topic Affinity (Top ", length(top_authors), " Authors)"),
  color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "#FDDBC7", "#CD661D", "#8B4513"))(100),
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  angle_col = 45,
  border_color = "grey90"
)
# Table: each top author's dominant topic
author_dominant <- dominant_topics %>%
  filter(Author %in% top_authors) %>%
  count(Author, dominant_topic, topic_label, sort = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(Author) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(dominant_topic, desc(n))

cat("Most prolific authors and their primary topic:\n\n")
print(author_dominant, n = 20)

33. Topic Diversity per Volume

Are some volumes thematically narrow (dominated by 1-2 topics) or broad? Shannon entropy measures diversity: higher = more diverse mix of topics.

# Calculate Shannon entropy per volume
vol_diversity <- make.dt(stm_model, meta = meta) %>%
  select(starts_with("Topic"), volume_num, year) %>%
  filter(!is.na(volume_num)) %>%
  group_by(volume_num) %>%
  summarize(
    across(starts_with("Topic"), mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    year = first(year),
    n_articles = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # Shannon entropy across topic proportions
    entropy = {
      props <- c_across(starts_with("Topic"))
      props <- props[props > 0]
      -sum(props * log(props))
    },
    # Normalized to 0-1 scale (max entropy = log(K))
    diversity = entropy / log(chosen_K)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(vol_diversity, aes(x = volume_num, y = diversity)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = diversity), show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(diversity, 2)), vjust = -0.3, size = 2.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Topic Diversity per Volume",
    subtitle = "Higher = more thematically diverse (Shannon entropy, normalized)",
    x = "Volume",
    y = "Diversity (0 = one topic dominates, 1 = perfectly even)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#FDDBC7", high = "#8B4513") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.1))

34. Ridgeline Plot - Topic Proportion Distributions

Shows the spread of topic proportions across all articles, not just averages. Some topics may have tight distributions (consistent presence) while others have long tails (a few articles are heavily about that topic).

library(ggridges)

ridge_data <- topic_article_link %>%
  mutate(topic_label_full = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", topic_label))

ggplot(ridge_data, aes(x = proportion, y = reorder(topic_label_full, topic_num),
                        fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2.5, rel_min_height = 0.01,
                                gradient_lwd = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#FDDBC7", high = "#8B4513", guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Topic Proportions Across Articles",
    subtitle = "Long right tails = some articles are strongly about that topic",
    x = "Topic Proportion",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, NA))

35. Topic Dendrogram

Hierarchical clustering showing which topics are most similar to each other based on their word distributions.

# Get topic-word matrix (beta)
topic_word_matrix <- exp(stm_model$beta$logbeta[[1]])

# Distance between topics based on word distributions
topic_dist <- dist(topic_word_matrix, method = "euclidean")
topic_clust <- hclust(topic_dist, method = "ward.D2")

# Rename labels
topic_clust$labels <- paste0("T", 1:chosen_K, ": ", topic_labels)

par(mar = c(2, 4, 3, 12))
plot(as.dendrogram(topic_clust),
     horiz = TRUE,
     main = "Topic Similarity Dendrogram",
     xlab = "Distance",
     cex = 0.8)
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))

36. Within-Topic Vocabulary Comparison (Early vs Late Period)

When the same topic appears across both eras of the Exponent, does the language shift? This reveals how the publication’s framing of shared concerns evolved over time.

cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("WITHIN-TOPIC VOCABULARY COMPARISON (EARLY vs LATE)\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Split into early and late eras at median year
median_year <- median(meta$year, na.rm = TRUE)
doc_topics_era <- doc_topics %>%
  mutate(era = ifelse(year <= median_year, "Early", "Late"))

cat("Era split at year", median_year, "\n")
cat("  Early:", sum(doc_topics_era$era == "Early"), "documents\n")
cat("  Late: ", sum(doc_topics_era$era == "Late"), "documents\n\n")

# Function: get word frequencies for top-quartile docs of a topic in an era
# (includes OCR junk filtering as safety net even though stopwords catch most)
get_topic_words_by_era <- function(topic_num, era_name) {
  topic_col <- paste0("Topic", topic_num)
  era_docs <- doc_topics_era %>% filter(era == era_name)
  threshold <- quantile(era_docs[[topic_col]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

  doc_ids <- era_docs %>%
    filter(.data[[topic_col]] >= threshold) %>%
    pull(doc_id)

  words <- processed_texts %>%
    filter(doc_id %in% doc_ids) %>%
    pull(text_final) %>%
    paste(collapse = " ") %>%
    str_split("\\s+") %>%
    `[[`(1)

  freq <- sort(table(words), decreasing = TRUE)
  tibble(word = names(freq), count = as.integer(freq)) %>%
    mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
    filter(
      nchar(word) >= 4,
      !word %in% all_stopwords,
      !is_ocr_fragment(word)
    )
}

# Pick the 6 most prevalent topics for comparison
top_6_for_vocab <- order(topic_props, decreasing = TRUE)[1:min(6, chosen_K)]

cat("Comparing vocabulary for top", length(top_6_for_vocab), "topics:\n")
for (t in top_6_for_vocab) {
  cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s\n", t, topic_labels[t]))
}
cat("\n")

# Build log-ratio plots
vocab_plots <- list()
for (idx in seq_along(top_6_for_vocab)) {
  t <- top_6_for_vocab[idx]

  words_early <- get_topic_words_by_era(t, "Early")
  words_late  <- get_topic_words_by_era(t, "Late")

  word_compare <- full_join(
    words_early %>% select(word, early_prop = proportion),
    words_late  %>% select(word, late_prop = proportion),
    by = "word"
  ) %>%
    replace_na(list(early_prop = 0.0001, late_prop = 0.0001)) %>%
    mutate(
      log_ratio = log2(early_prop / late_prop),
      avg_prop = (early_prop + late_prop) / 2
    ) %>%
    filter(avg_prop > 0.001) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(log_ratio)))

  # Top 20 words each direction
  top_early <- word_compare %>% filter(log_ratio > 0) %>% head(20)
  top_late  <- word_compare %>% filter(log_ratio < 0) %>% head(20)
  plot_words <- bind_rows(top_early, top_late) %>%
    mutate(
      direction = ifelse(log_ratio > 0,
                         paste0("More in Early (\u2264", median_year, ")"),
                         paste0("More in Late (>", median_year, ")")),
      word = reorder(word, log_ratio)
    )

  early_label <- paste0("More in Early (\u2264", median_year, ")")
  late_label  <- paste0("More in Late (>", median_year, ")")

  p <- ggplot(plot_words, aes(x = log_ratio, y = word, fill = direction)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c("#CD661D", "#8B4513"),
                                        c(early_label, late_label))) +
    labs(title = paste0("T", t, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels[t], 35)),
         x = "Word emphasis by period: Right= Before 1877 and Left= After 1877", y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"))

  vocab_plots[[idx]] <- p
}

# Combine plots
if (length(vocab_plots) >= 6) {
  (vocab_plots[[1]] | vocab_plots[[2]] | vocab_plots[[3]]) /
  (vocab_plots[[4]] | vocab_plots[[5]] | vocab_plots[[6]]) +
    plot_annotation(
      title = "Within-Topic Vocabulary Shifts: Early vs Late Period",
      subtitle = paste0("Top 20 words each era emphasizes differently (split at ",
                        median_year, ")"),
      theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
    )
} else if (length(vocab_plots) >= 1) {
  wrap_plots(vocab_plots, ncol = min(3, length(vocab_plots))) +
    plot_annotation(title = "Within-Topic Vocabulary Shifts: Early vs Late Period")
}
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("WITHIN-TOPIC VOCABULARY COMPARISON (EARLY vs LATE) — paginated\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Split into early and late eras at median year
median_year <- median(meta$year, na.rm = TRUE)
doc_topics_era <- doc_topics %>%
  mutate(era = ifelse(year <= median_year, "Early", "Late"))

cat("Era split at year", median_year, "\n")
cat("  Early:", sum(doc_topics_era$era == "Early"), "documents\n")
cat("  Late: ", sum(doc_topics_era$era == "Late"), "documents\n\n")

# Function: get word frequencies for top-quartile docs of a topic in an era
get_topic_words_by_era <- function(topic_num, era_name) {
  topic_col <- paste0("Topic", topic_num)
  era_docs <- doc_topics_era %>% filter(era == era_name)
  threshold <- quantile(era_docs[[topic_col]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

  doc_ids <- era_docs %>%
    filter(.data[[topic_col]] >= threshold) %>%
    pull(doc_id)

  words <- processed_texts %>%
    filter(doc_id %in% doc_ids) %>%
    pull(text_final) %>%
    paste(collapse = " ") %>%
    str_split("\\s+") %>%
    `[[`(1)

  freq <- sort(table(words), decreasing = TRUE)
  tibble(word = names(freq), count = as.integer(freq)) %>%
    mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
    filter(
      nchar(word) >= 4,
      !word %in% all_stopwords,
      !is_ocr_fragment(word)
    )
}

# All topics ordered by prevalence (most prevalent first)
all_topics_ordered <- order(topic_props, decreasing = TRUE)

cat("Building plots for all", length(all_topics_ordered), "topics...\n")

# Build a plot for every topic at GENEROUS sizing
vocab_plots <- list()
for (idx in seq_along(all_topics_ordered)) {
  t <- all_topics_ordered[idx]

  words_early <- get_topic_words_by_era(t, "Early")
  words_late  <- get_topic_words_by_era(t, "Late")

  word_compare <- full_join(
    words_early %>% select(word, early_prop = proportion),
    words_late  %>% select(word, late_prop  = proportion),
    by = "word"
  ) %>%
    replace_na(list(early_prop = 0.0001, late_prop = 0.0001)) %>%
    mutate(
      log_ratio = log2(early_prop / late_prop),
      avg_prop  = (early_prop + late_prop) / 2
    ) %>%
    filter(avg_prop > 0.001) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(log_ratio)))

  top_early <- word_compare %>% filter(log_ratio > 0) %>% head(20)
  top_late  <- word_compare %>% filter(log_ratio < 0) %>% head(20)

  early_label <- paste0("More in Early (\u2264", median_year, ")")
  late_label  <- paste0("More in Late (>",    median_year, ")")

  plot_words <- bind_rows(top_early, top_late) %>%
    mutate(
      direction = ifelse(log_ratio > 0, early_label, late_label),
      word      = reorder(word, log_ratio)
    )

  p <- ggplot(plot_words, aes(x = log_ratio, y = word, fill = direction)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c("#CD661D", "#8B4513"),
                                        c(early_label, late_label))) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("T", t, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels[t], 45)),
      x     = paste0("\u2190 More after ", median_year,
                     "   |   More before ", median_year, " \u2192"),
      y     = NULL,
      fill  = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title      = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.margin     = margin(8, 12, 8, 12)
    )

  vocab_plots[[idx]] <- p
}

# Paginate: 5 plots per page, 2 columns wide (2+2+1 per page)
plots_per_page <- 4
n_pages <- ceiling(length(vocab_plots) / plots_per_page)

for (page in seq_len(n_pages)) {
  start_i <- (page - 1) * plots_per_page + 1
  end_i   <- min(start_i + plots_per_page - 1, length(vocab_plots))
  batch   <- vocab_plots[start_i:end_i]

  combined <- wrap_plots(batch, ncol = 2)   # already 2, just produces 2×2 with 4 plots +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Within-Topic Vocabulary Shifts — Page ",
                        page, " of ", n_pages),
      subtitle = paste0("Topics ", start_i, "\u2013", end_i,
                        " by prevalence rank. Era split at ", median_year,
                        ". Orange = more before; brown = more after."),
      theme    = theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
                       plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))
    )

  print(combined)

  # Save each page as a standalone PNG (so dissertation renders at full size,
  # not the notebook's compressed display width).
  while (!is.null(dev.list())) dev.off()
  ggsave(
    file.path(output_path,
              paste0("vocab_shifts_K", chosen_K, "_page", page, ".png")),
    combined, width = 14, height = 24, dpi = 200, limitsize = FALSE,
    device = ragg::agg_png
  )
}

cat("\nSaved", n_pages, "PNG pages to:", output_path, "\n")

##USING JSD TO IDENTIFY THE CHANGE OF YEAR TO A SPECIFIC TOPIC Y AXIS RATHER THAN NORMALIZING AND USING A MEDIAN YEAR FOR THE CHANGE IN WORD USAGE

cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("WITHIN-TOPIC VOCABULARY CHANGEPOINT DETECTION — paginated\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# ---- Helpers (reused below for trajectory chunk too) ----
.topic_words <- function(doc_ids) {
  words <- processed_texts %>%
    filter(doc_id %in% doc_ids) %>%
    pull(text_final) %>%
    paste(collapse = " ") %>%
    str_split("\\s+") %>%
    `[[`(1)
  words[nchar(words) >= 4 &
        !words %in% all_stopwords &
        !is_ocr_fragment(words)]
}

.js_divergence <- function(words_a, words_b) {
  if (length(words_a) < 50 || length(words_b) < 50) return(0)
  vocab_union <- union(unique(words_a), unique(words_b))
  p_a <- as.numeric(table(factor(words_a, levels = vocab_union))) / length(words_a)
  p_b <- as.numeric(table(factor(words_b, levels = vocab_union))) / length(words_b)
  m   <- (p_a + p_b) / 2
  eps <- 1e-12
  kl  <- function(p, q) sum(p * log2((p + eps) / (q + eps)), na.rm = TRUE)
  (kl(p_a, m) + kl(p_b, m)) / 2
}

find_topic_changepoint <- function(topic_num, min_docs_per_side = 15) {
  topic_col <- paste0("Topic", topic_num)
  threshold <- quantile(doc_topics[[topic_col]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

  topic_docs <- doc_topics %>%
    filter(.data[[topic_col]] >= threshold, !is.na(year)) %>%
    select(doc_id, year) %>%
    arrange(year)

  if (nrow(topic_docs) < 2 * min_docs_per_side) {
    return(list(year = NA_integer_, max_div = NA_real_, curve = NULL))
  }

  candidates <- sort(unique(topic_docs$year))
  candidates <- candidates[
    vapply(candidates, function(y) {
      sum(topic_docs$year <= y) >= min_docs_per_side &
      sum(topic_docs$year >  y) >= min_docs_per_side
    }, logical(1))
  ]
  if (length(candidates) == 0) {
    return(list(year = NA_integer_, max_div = NA_real_, curve = NULL))
  }

  divergences <- vapply(candidates, function(yr) {
    early_ids <- topic_docs$doc_id[topic_docs$year <= yr]
    late_ids  <- topic_docs$doc_id[topic_docs$year >  yr]
    .js_divergence(.topic_words(early_ids), .topic_words(late_ids))
  }, numeric(1))

  list(
    year    = candidates[which.max(divergences)],
    max_div = max(divergences),
    curve   = tibble(year = candidates, divergence = divergences)
  )
}

# ---- Detect changepoint for every topic (cache to .rds so trajectory chunk reuses) ----
cat("Detecting changepoints for all", chosen_K, "topics...\n")
changepoints <- list()
for (t in 1:chosen_K) {
  cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s ... ", t, str_trunc(topic_labels[t], 35)))
  cp <- find_topic_changepoint(t)
  changepoints[[t]] <- cp
  if (is.na(cp$year)) {
    cat("(too few docs)\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("split at %d (JS = %.3f)\n", cp$year, cp$max_div))
  }
}

# Save for trajectory chunk
saveRDS(changepoints,
        file.path(output_path, paste0("changepoints_K", chosen_K, ".rds")))

# Summary CSV
changepoint_df <- tibble(
  topic_num   = 1:chosen_K,
  label       = topic_labels,
  prevalence  = topic_props,
  split_year  = vapply(changepoints, `[[`, integer(1),  "year"),
  max_jsd     = vapply(changepoints, `[[`, numeric(1), "max_div")
) %>%
  arrange(desc(prevalence))

cat("\nChangepoint summary (sorted by prevalence):\n")
print(changepoint_df, n = Inf)

write_csv(changepoint_df,
          file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_changepoints_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))

# ---- Word frequencies for a topic on one side of its changepoint ----
get_topic_words_at_split <- function(topic_num, split_year, side = c("early","late")) {
  side <- match.arg(side)
  topic_col <- paste0("Topic", topic_num)
  threshold <- quantile(doc_topics[[topic_col]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

  era_filter <- if (side == "early") rlang::expr(year <= !!split_year)
                else                  rlang::expr(year >  !!split_year)

  doc_ids <- doc_topics %>%
    filter(.data[[topic_col]] >= threshold, !is.na(year)) %>%
    filter(!!era_filter) %>%
    pull(doc_id)

  words <- .topic_words(doc_ids)
  freq  <- sort(table(words), decreasing = TRUE)
  tibble(word = names(freq), count = as.integer(freq)) %>%
    mutate(proportion = count / sum(count))
}

# ---- Build a plot per topic at GENEROUS sizing ----
all_topics_ordered <- order(topic_props, decreasing = TRUE)

vocab_plots <- list()
for (idx in seq_along(all_topics_ordered)) {
  t  <- all_topics_ordered[idx]
  cp <- changepoints[[t]]

  if (is.na(cp$year)) {
    vocab_plots[[idx]] <- ggplot() +
      ggplot::annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0,
               label = paste0("T", t, ": insufficient data"),
               size = 6) +
      theme_void()
    next
  }

  split_yr <- cp$year
  words_early <- get_topic_words_at_split(t, split_yr, "early")
  words_late  <- get_topic_words_at_split(t, split_yr, "late")

  word_compare <- full_join(
    words_early %>% select(word, early_prop = proportion),
    words_late  %>% select(word, late_prop  = proportion),
    by = "word"
  ) %>%
    replace_na(list(early_prop = 0.0001, late_prop = 0.0001)) %>%
    mutate(
      log_ratio = log2(early_prop / late_prop),
      avg_prop  = (early_prop + late_prop) / 2
    ) %>%
    filter(avg_prop > 0.001) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(log_ratio)))

  top_early <- word_compare %>% filter(log_ratio > 0) %>% head(15)
top_late  <- word_compare %>% filter(log_ratio < 0) %>% head(15)

  early_label <- paste0("More \u2264", split_yr)
  late_label  <- paste0("More >",     split_yr)

  plot_words <- bind_rows(top_early, top_late) %>%
    mutate(
      direction = ifelse(log_ratio > 0, early_label, late_label),
      word      = reorder(word, log_ratio)
    )

  p <- ggplot(plot_words, aes(x = log_ratio, y = word, fill = direction)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c("#CD661D", "#8B4513"),
                                        c(early_label, late_label))) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("T", t, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels[t], 45)),
      subtitle = paste0("Changepoint: ", split_yr,
                        "   |   JSD = ", sprintf("%.2f", cp$max_div),
                        "   |   Prevalence: ",
                        sprintf("%.1f%%", topic_props[t] * 100)),
      x        = paste0("\u2190 after ", split_yr,
                        "   |   before ", split_yr, " \u2192"),
      y        = NULL, fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title      = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey35"),
      axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.margin     = margin(8, 12, 8, 12)
    )

  vocab_plots[[idx]] <- p
}

# Paginate: 5 plots per page, 2 columns wide
plots_per_page <- 4
n_pages <- ceiling(length(vocab_plots) / plots_per_page)

for (page in seq_len(n_pages)) {
  start_i <- (page - 1) * plots_per_page + 1
  end_i   <- min(start_i + plots_per_page - 1, length(vocab_plots))
  batch   <- vocab_plots[start_i:end_i]

  combined <- wrap_plots(batch, ncol = 2) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Topic-Specific Vocabulary Changepoints — Page ",
                        page, " of ", n_pages),
      subtitle = paste0("Topics ", start_i, "\u2013", end_i,
                        " by prevalence rank. Each split at its own ",
                        "JSD-maximizing year."),
      theme    = theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
                       plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))
    )

  print(combined)

  ggsave(
    file.path(output_path,
              paste0("changepoint_shifts_K", chosen_K, "_page", page, ".png")),
    combined, width = 14, height = 22, dpi = 200, limitsize = FALSE,
    device = ragg::agg_png
  )
}

cat("\nSaved", n_pages, "PNG pages to:", output_path, "\n")

##WORDS CHANGE OVER TIME USING JENSEN-SHANNON DIVERGENCE TO MARK CHANGE OVER TIME RELATIVE TO EACH TOPIC

cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("PER-TOPIC WORD TRAJECTORIES OVER TIME\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Reload changepoints if not in memory (from Chunk A)
if (!exists("changepoints")) {
  changepoints <- readRDS(
    file.path(output_path, paste0("changepoints_K", chosen_K, ".rds"))
  )
}

# ---- Compute trajectories for one topic ----
# For each of the topic's top N words, the proportion of that word's
# occurrences across all topic-tokens in each year. Smoothed with loess
# to dampen year-to-year noise. Limited to words that have substantive
# presence (avoid plotting words that appear in only 1-2 years).
get_word_trajectories <- function(topic_num, n_words = 8, min_years_present = 4) {
  topic_col <- paste0("Topic", topic_num)
  threshold <- quantile(doc_topics[[topic_col]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

  topic_doc_info <- doc_topics %>%
    filter(.data[[topic_col]] >= threshold, !is.na(year)) %>%
    select(doc_id, year)

  if (nrow(topic_doc_info) < 30) return(NULL)

  # Tokenize all top-quartile docs, tagged with year
  word_year_df <- processed_texts %>%
    filter(doc_id %in% topic_doc_info$doc_id) %>%
    select(doc_id, text_final) %>%
    inner_join(topic_doc_info, by = "doc_id") %>%
    mutate(words = str_split(text_final, "\\s+")) %>%
    select(year, words) %>%
    unnest(words) %>%
    filter(nchar(words) >= 4,
           !words %in% all_stopwords,
           !is_ocr_fragment(words))

  # Top N most frequent words overall in the topic — but require they
  # appear in at least min_years_present distinct years (avoids one-shot OCR fluke)
  word_year_counts <- word_year_df %>%
    count(year, words) %>%
    group_by(words) %>%
    mutate(years_present = n_distinct(year),
           total = sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup()

  top_words <- word_year_counts %>%
    filter(years_present >= min_years_present) %>%
    distinct(words, total) %>%
    arrange(desc(total)) %>%
    head(n_words) %>%
    pull(words)

  if (length(top_words) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Per-year total of any top-word tokens (denominator)
  per_year_totals <- word_year_df %>%
    filter(words %in% top_words) %>%
    count(year, name = "year_total")

  trajectory <- word_year_counts %>%
    filter(words %in% top_words) %>%
    inner_join(per_year_totals, by = "year") %>%
    mutate(prop = n / year_total) %>%
    select(year, word = words, prop)

  # Fill missing year×word combos with zero so lines drop to 0 where word absent
  all_years <- seq(min(trajectory$year), max(trajectory$year))
  trajectory <- trajectory %>%
    complete(year = all_years, word = top_words, fill = list(prop = 0))

  trajectory
}

# ---- Build a trajectory plot per topic ----
all_topics_ordered <- order(topic_props, decreasing = TRUE)

# Distinct color palette that can render 8 lines clearly
traj_palette <- c("#CD661D", "#1F77B4", "#2CA02C", "#9467BD",
                  "#8B4513", "#17BECF", "#E377C2", "#7F7F7F")

trajectory_plots <- list()
for (idx in seq_along(all_topics_ordered)) {
  t  <- all_topics_ordered[idx]
  cp <- changepoints[[t]]

  cat(sprintf("  T%d (%s): computing trajectories...\n",
              t, str_trunc(topic_labels[t], 35)))
  traj <- get_word_trajectories(t, n_words = 8, min_years_present = 4)

  if (is.null(traj) || nrow(traj) == 0) {
    trajectory_plots[[idx]] <- ggplot() +
      ggplot::annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0,
               label = paste0("T", t, ": insufficient data"),
               size = 6) +
      theme_void()
    next
  }

  p <- ggplot(traj, aes(x = year, y = prop, color = word)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, span = 0.55, linewidth = 1.1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = traj_palette[seq_len(n_distinct(traj$word))]) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 6)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("T", t, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels[t], 45)),
      subtitle = if (!is.na(cp$year))
                   paste0("Changepoint: ", cp$year,
                          "   |   JSD = ", sprintf("%.2f", cp$max_div))
                 else "Changepoint: (insufficient data)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Share of topic vocab",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.text     = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.8, "lines"),
      plot.title      = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey35"),
      axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin     = margin(8, 12, 8, 12)
    )

  # Overlay the JSD changepoint year as a dashed vertical line
  if (!is.na(cp$year)) {
    p <- p +
      geom_vline(xintercept = cp$year,
                 linetype = "dashed", color = "grey25", linewidth = 0.6) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = cp$year, y = Inf,
               label = paste0(" CP: ", cp$year),
               hjust = 0, vjust = 1.5, size = 3.2, color = "grey25",
               fontface = "italic")
  }

  trajectory_plots[[idx]] <- p
}

# Paginate: 5 plots per page, 2 columns wide
plots_per_page <- 4
n_pages <- ceiling(length(trajectory_plots) / plots_per_page)

for (page in seq_len(n_pages)) {
  start_i <- (page - 1) * plots_per_page + 1
  end_i   <- min(start_i + plots_per_page - 1, length(trajectory_plots))
  batch   <- trajectory_plots[start_i:end_i]

  combined <- wrap_plots(batch, ncol = 2) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Top-Word Trajectories Within Topics — Page ",
                        page, " of ", n_pages),
      subtitle = paste0("Topics ", start_i, "\u2013", end_i,
                        " by prevalence rank. Lines = LOESS-smoothed share ",
                        "of topic vocab per year. Dashed line = JSD changepoint."),
      theme    = theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
                       plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))
    )

  print(combined)

  ggsave(
    file.path(output_path,
              paste0("word_trajectories_K", chosen_K, "_page", page, ".png")),
    combined, width = 14, height = 20, dpi = 200, limitsize = FALSE,
    device = ragg::agg_png
  )
}

cat("\nSaved", n_pages, "PNG pages to:", output_path, "\n")

37. Topic Diversity & Concentration (Document-Level)

Section 33 measures diversity per volume. This section measures it per document: is the typical article about one topic or several?

cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("DOCUMENT-LEVEL TOPIC DIVERSITY\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Shannon entropy per document (higher = more diverse)
doc_entropy <- doc_topics %>%
  select(doc_id, starts_with("Topic")) %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("Topic"), names_to = "topic", values_to = "p") %>%
  filter(p > 0) %>%
  group_by(doc_id) %>%
  summarize(entropy = -sum(p * log2(p)), .groups = "drop")

# Gini concentration per document
doc_gini <- doc_topics %>%
  select(doc_id, starts_with("Topic")) %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("Topic"), names_to = "topic", values_to = "p") %>%
  group_by(doc_id) %>%
  summarize(
    max_topic_prop = max(p),
    gini = 1 - sum(p^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Summary
diversity_summary <- doc_entropy %>%
  left_join(doc_gini, by = "doc_id") %>%
  summarize(
    mean_entropy = mean(entropy),
    sd_entropy = sd(entropy),
    mean_gini = mean(gini),
    mean_max_prop = mean(max_topic_prop),
    pct_strong_exemplar = mean(max_topic_prop >= 0.4) * 100
  )

cat("Diversity Metrics (Woman's Exponent):\n\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean Shannon Entropy:     %.2f bits\n", diversity_summary$mean_entropy))
cat(sprintf("  SD Entropy:               %.2f\n", diversity_summary$sd_entropy))
cat(sprintf("  Mean Gini (inv-Herf):     %.3f\n", diversity_summary$mean_gini))
cat(sprintf("  Mean Dominant Topic %%:    %.1f%%\n", diversity_summary$mean_max_prop * 100))
cat(sprintf("  Strong Exemplars (>40%%):  %.1f%% of documents\n\n", diversity_summary$pct_strong_exemplar))

# Entropy distribution
p1 <- ggplot(doc_entropy, aes(x = entropy)) +
  geom_density(fill = "#CD661D", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.8, color = "#8B4513") +
  labs(title = "Topic Diversity per Document",
       subtitle = "Higher entropy = article discusses more topics evenly",
       x = "Shannon Entropy (bits)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Dominant topic concentration
p2 <- ggplot(doc_gini, aes(x = max_topic_prop)) +
  geom_density(fill = "#CD661D", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.8, color = "#8B4513") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.4, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.42, y = 0, label = "Exemplar\nthreshold", size = 3, hjust = 0) +
  labs(title = "Topic Concentration per Document",
       subtitle = "Higher = article dominated by single topic; dashed line = 40% exemplar threshold",
       x = "Dominant Topic Proportion", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format())

p1 / p2

Effective Number of Topics

# Publication-level topic distribution
pub_topic_dist <- doc_topics %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_num = as.integer(str_remove(topic, "Topic")),
    label = str_trunc(topic_labels[topic_num], 25)
  )

# Effective number of topics = exp(entropy of topic distribution)
eff_topics <- pub_topic_dist %>%
  filter(prevalence > 0) %>%
  summarize(eff = exp(-sum(prevalence * log(prevalence)))) %>%
  pull(eff)

cat(sprintf("Effective number of topics: %.1f (of %d)\n\n", eff_topics, chosen_K))

ggplot(pub_topic_dist, aes(x = reorder(label, -prevalence), y = prevalence)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#CD661D", alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Topic Weight Distribution \u2014 Woman's Exponent",
       subtitle = paste0("Effective topics: ", round(eff_topics, 1), " of ", chosen_K),
       x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())

38. Topic Prevalence Heatmap Over Time

# Yearly topic prevalence
yearly_topics_we <- doc_topics %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  select(year, starts_with("Topic")) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("Topic"), names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_num = as.integer(str_remove(topic, "Topic")),
    short_label = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(topic_labels[topic_num], 20))
  )

ggplot(yearly_topics_we, aes(x = year, y = short_label, fill = prevalence)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#8B4513",
                      labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(title = "Topic Prevalence Over Time \u2014 Woman's Exponent",
       subtitle = "Darker = higher prevalence in that year",
       x = "Year", y = NULL, fill = "Prevalence") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(yearly_topics_we$year)))

39. Topic Percentage Change (Early vs Late Era)

How much does each topic’s prevalence shift from the early period to the late period?

cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("PERCENTAGE CHANGE IN TOPICS: EARLY vs LATE ERA\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Mean prevalence per topic per era
era_means <- doc_topics_era %>%
  group_by(era) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(-era, names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(topic_num = as.integer(str_remove(topic, "Topic"))) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = era, values_from = prevalence)

era_change <- era_means %>%
  mutate(
    label = topic_labels[topic_num],
    early_pct = Early * 100,
    late_pct = Late * 100,
    abs_change = late_pct - early_pct,
    pct_change = ifelse(Early > 0.001,
                        (Late - Early) / Early * 100,
                        NA_real_),
    direction = case_when(
      abs_change > 0.5  ~ "Grew in Late Period",
      abs_change < -0.5 ~ "Declined in Late Period",
      TRUE ~ "Stable"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_change))

cat(sprintf("Era split: Early (\u2264%d) vs Late (>%d)\n\n", median_year, median_year))
cat(sprintf("%-35s  %7s  %7s  %8s  %9s\n",
            "Topic", "Early%", "Late%", "Change", "% Change"))
cat(rep("-", 80), "\n", sep = "")
for (i in 1:nrow(era_change)) {
  r <- era_change[i, ]
  pct_str <- ifelse(is.na(r$pct_change), "N/A", sprintf("%+.0f%%", r$pct_change))
  cat(sprintf("%-35s  %5.1f%%  %5.1f%%  %+6.1fpp  %9s\n",
              str_trunc(r$label, 35), r$early_pct, r$late_pct, r$abs_change, pct_str))
}

# Absolute change bar chart
p_abs <- era_change %>%
  mutate(display = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(label, 28)),
         display = reorder(display, abs_change)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = abs_change, y = display, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Grew in Late Period" = "#458B74",
                                "Declined in Late Period" = "#CD661D",
                                "Stable" = "#94a3b8")) +
  labs(title = paste0("Topic Change: Early (\u2264", median_year,
                      ") vs Late (>", median_year, ") Period"),
       subtitle = "Absolute change in percentage points of topic prevalence",
       x = "Change (percentage points)", y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

print(p_abs)
# Relative (%) change bar chart
era_change_filtered <- era_change %>%
  filter(!is.na(pct_change), abs(pct_change) < 500) %>%
  mutate(display = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(label, 28)),
         display = reorder(display, pct_change))

if (nrow(era_change_filtered) > 0) {
  ggplot(era_change_filtered, aes(x = pct_change, y = display, fill = direction)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Grew in Late Period" = "#458B74",
                                  "Declined in Late Period" = "#CD661D",
                                  "Stable" = "#94a3b8")) +
    labs(title = "Relative Percentage Change in Topic Prevalence",
         subtitle = paste0("How much did each topic grow or shrink relative to its early-period (\u2264",
                           median_year, ") baseline?"),
         x = "Relative Change (%)", y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())
}

40. Topic Evolution Summary

cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("TOPIC EVOLUTION SUMMARY\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

topic_evolution <- era_change %>%
  mutate(
    evolution_type = case_when(
      abs(abs_change) <= 0.5 ~ "Stable",
      abs_change > 0.5  ~ "Growing",
      abs_change < -0.5 ~ "Declining"
    ),
    evolution_type = factor(evolution_type,
                            levels = c("Stable", "Growing", "Declining"))
  )

cat("TOPIC EVOLUTION CLASSIFICATION:\n\n")
for (etype in c("Stable", "Growing", "Declining")) {
  cat(toupper(etype), ":\n")
  subset_ev <- topic_evolution %>% filter(evolution_type == etype)
  if (nrow(subset_ev) > 0) {
    for (i in 1:nrow(subset_ev)) {
      r <- subset_ev[i, ]
      cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s (Early: %.1f%%, Late: %.1f%%, %+.1fpp)\n",
                  r$topic_num, r$label, r$early_pct, r$late_pct, r$abs_change))
    }
  } else {
    cat("  (none)\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
}

# Scatter plot: early vs late prevalence
ggplot(topic_evolution, aes(x = early_pct, y = late_pct, color = evolution_type)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "grey70", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(label, 25))),
                  size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Stable" = "#6495ED",
    "Growing" = "#458B74",
    "Declining" = "#CD661D"
  )) +
  labs(title = "Topic Evolution: Early vs Late Period",
       subtitle = paste0("Diagonal = no change | Above = grew in late period (",
                         median_year + 1, "-", max(meta$year, na.rm = TRUE),
                         ") | Below = declined"),
       x = paste0("Early Period Mean % (\u2264", median_year, ")"),
       y = paste0("Late Period Mean % (>", median_year, ")"),
       color = "Evolution Type") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16)) +
  coord_fixed()

41. Representative Passages

Extracts the most representative sentences from top articles for each topic – ready to quote in your dissertation.

cat("REPRESENTATIVE PASSAGES PER TOPIC\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# For each topic, get top 3 articles, extract best sentences
for (t in 1:chosen_K) {
  cat("TOPIC", t, ":", topic_labels[t], "\n")
  cat(rep("-", 60), "\n", sep = "")

  # Get top 3 articles for this topic
  top_docs <- topic_article_link %>%
    filter(topic_num == t) %>%
    head(3)

  # Get the topic's top words for sentence scoring
  topic_words <- tolower(all_top_words$frex[t, 1:15])

  for (j in 1:nrow(top_docs)) {
    fname <- top_docs$filename[j]
    title_str <- ifelse(is.na(top_docs$Title[j]), fname, top_docs$Title[j])

    # Find and read the file
    match_file <- list.files(input_path, pattern = fixed(fname),
                             recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
    if (length(match_file) == 0) next

    full_text <- read_file(match_file[1])

    # Split into sentences, score by topic word density
    sentences <- str_split(full_text, "(?<=[.!?])\\s+")[[1]]
    sentences <- sentences[nchar(sentences) > 40 & nchar(sentences) < 500]

    if (length(sentences) == 0) next

    scores <- sapply(sentences, function(s) {
      words <- tolower(str_split(s, "\\s+")[[1]])
      sum(words %in% topic_words) / max(length(words), 1)
    })

    best_idx <- order(scores, decreasing = TRUE)[1]
    best_sentence <- str_squish(sentences[best_idx])

    cat(sprintf("\n  [%s] (%.1f%% Topic %d)\n",
                title_str, top_docs$proportion[j] * 100, t))
    cat("  \"", str_trunc(best_sentence, 300), "\"\n", sep = "")
  }
  cat("\n\n")
}

42. Export Results

# 1. Full topic-article link (all documents x all topics)
full_export <- topic_article_link %>%
  mutate(proportion_pct = round(proportion * 100, 2))

write_csv(full_export,
          file.path(output_path, "topic_article_full_link.csv"))

# 2. Top articles per topic (best exemplars)
exemplars <- topic_article_link %>%
  group_by(topic_num, topic_label) %>%
  slice_max(proportion, n = 25) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(proportion_pct = round(proportion * 100, 2))

write_csv(exemplars,
          file.path(output_path, "topic_exemplar_articles.csv"))

# 3. Topic summary with labels and words
topic_summary <- tibble(
  topic_num = 1:chosen_K,
  label = topic_labels,
  prevalence_pct = round(topic_props * 100, 2),
  frex_words = apply(all_top_words$frex, 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", ")),
  prob_words = apply(all_top_words$prob, 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
)

write_csv(topic_summary,
          file.path(output_path, "topic_summary.csv"))

# 4. Dominant topic assignments (one row per article)
write_csv(dominant_topics,
          file.path(output_path, "dominant_topic_per_article.csv"))

# 5. Strong exemplars only
strong_only <- dominant_topics %>% filter(strong_exemplar)
write_csv(strong_only,
          file.path(output_path, "strong_exemplars.csv"))

cat("Exported to:", output_path, "\n")
cat("  - topic_article_full_link.csv (all doc-topic proportions)\n")
cat("  - topic_exemplar_articles.csv (top 25 articles per topic)\n")
cat("  - topic_summary.csv (labels + words)\n")
cat("  - dominant_topic_per_article.csv (each article tagged with its strongest topic)\n")
cat("  - strong_exemplars.csv (articles >", exemplar_threshold * 100, "% in their dominant topic)\n")

43. Read a Specific Article

Use this to quickly read the text of any article you’ve identified as an exemplar.

read_article <- function(fname, input_dir = input_path) {
  # Find the file
  match <- list.files(input_dir, pattern = fixed(fname), recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
  if (length(match) == 0) {
    cat("File not found:", fname, "\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  txt <- read_file(match[1])
  # Print first 500 chars
  cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
  cat("File:", fname, "\n")
  cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")
  cat(substr(txt, 1, 2000), "\n\n...")
  return(invisible(txt))
}

# Example: read the top article from Topic 1
top1 <- topic_article_link %>% filter(topic_num == 1) %>% head(1)
read_article(top1$filename)

44. Save Session

save(stm_model, docs, vocab, meta, raw_texts, processed_texts,
     all_stopwords, topic_labels, topic_article_link,
     dominant_topics, inventory_subset,
     file = file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_linker_session_K", chosen_K, ".RData")))

cat("Session saved. Reload with:\n")
cat('  load("', file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_linker_session_K", chosen_K, ".RData")), '")\n', sep = "")

Quick Reference

After running this notebook, use the exported CSVs:

  • topic_exemplar_articles.csv - Open this to see the top 25 articles for each topic. These are your best evidentiary examples.
  • topic_article_full_link.csv - Full matrix if you need to find any article’s topic distribution.
  • topic_summary.csv - Quick reference for topic labels and key words.

To read a specific article in R:

read_article("WE_Vol32_1903_Issue1_002_WOMANHOOD.txt")
